compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/bare soil. Only P3 remains as a viable specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Bare soil observed in yellow pot. No plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Bare soil observed in black pot. No plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows significant chlorosis and structural decline. Leaf turgor is poor.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of Silver Guest specimen.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3, likely an intervention for calcium or pH adjustment.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of white rabbit anchor in P3. No incidental growth or moss detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: Over the 5-day sequence, the Pothos (P3) has undergone progressive senescence. The leaf has lost structural integrity and color vibrancy. The other pots have remained devoid of vegetation throughout the observation window.

visual_health_inference.P3_health: Critical. The specimen is exhibiting signs of severe stress, likely due to root zone compromise or light deficiency.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: The biome is failing. The lack of specimens in P1, P2, and P4 suggests a total collapse of the intended botanical registry.

anomalies: White rabbit figurine (scale anchor) present in P3.
Systemic absence of P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Only the Pothos (P3) remains, and it is in a state of advanced decline. The soil in all pots appears dark and potentially waterlogged. The intervention of eggshells in P3 has not prevented the observed chlorosis. The environment is currently unsuitable for the sustained health of the registered species.
